7.description: Tennessee 211 is a statewide or regional referral resource for Tennesseans affected by the criminal justice system. Free statewide information and referral service connecting Tennesseans to health and human services including housing, food, utilities, employment, and crisis support. United Way–supported navigators help callers find local programs by need and ZIP code. Tennessee 211 is a referral line—not a direct-service provider. Users can search by location, service type, or need to find active providers in throughout Tennessee. Specialists or online tools help match people to housing, treatment, employment, legal aid, or benefits programs operated by other organizations. This entry describes a navigation hub—staff here do not replace direct services at partner agencies.
7.description_es: Tennessee 211 es un recurso de referencia estatal o regional para tennesseanos afectados por el sistema de justicia penal. Los usuarios pueden buscar por ubicación, tipo de servicio o necesidad para encontrar proveedores activos en en todo Tennessee. Herramientas en línea o especialistas ayudan a conectar con vivienda, tratamiento, empleo, asistencia legal o beneficios operados por otras organizaciones. Esta entrada describe un centro de navegación—el personal no reemplaza servicios directos en agencias aliadas.

12.description: West Tennessee Legal Services — Statewide Intake serves justice-involved adults and families rebuilding stability after incarceration in throughout Tennessee. Nonprofit civil legal aid serving 17 West Tennessee counties including Memphis, Jackson, and surrounding rural communities with housing, benefits, family law, and consumer matters affecting reentry stability. Call 800-372-8346 for intake; Memphis-area cases served from regional offices. Does not handle criminal cases. Representation is limited to civil legal matters—not active criminal cases. Income, household size, and offense type determine eligibility for record relief. Contact the program to confirm current intake steps, required documents, and whether walk-ins or referrals are accepted.
12.description_es: West Tennessee Legal Services — Statewide Intake ofrece asistencia legal civil gratuita o de bajo costo para residentes de bajos ingresos en en todo Tennessee. Los abogados ayudan con sellado o eliminación de antecedentes, desalojos, beneficios y otros asuntos civiles—no casos penales activos. La representación se limita a asuntos legales civiles—no casos penales activos. Ingresos, tamaño del hogar y tipo de delito determinan la elegibilidad para alivio de antecedentes. Contacte la oficina para confirmar pasos de admisión, documentos requeridos y si aceptan visitas sin cita o referencias.

13.description: Memphis Area Legal Services — Intake serves justice-involved adults and families rebuilding stability after incarceration in Shelby County. Nonprofit civil legal aid historically serving Shelby County low-income residents with housing, domestic violence, foreclosure, and benefits matters. Verify current intake routing with Help4TN or WTLS as West Tennessee Legal Services expanded Memphis-area coverage in 2024. Does not provide criminal defense representation. Representation is limited to civil legal matters—not active criminal cases. Income, household size, and offense type determine eligibility for record relief. Contact the program to confirm current intake steps, required documents, and whether walk-ins or referrals are accepted.

14.description: TN Department of Human Services — Benefits Portal is a statewide or regional referral resource for Tennesseans affected by the criminal justice system. Official statewide portal for applying for and managing Tennessee public benefits including TennCare Medicaid, SNAP food assistance, TANF cash assistance, and child care subsidies. Justice-involved individuals can apply for health coverage and food support after release. County DHS offices assist with verification and redetermination. Users can search by location, service type, or need to find active providers in throughout Tennessee. Specialists or online tools help match people to housing, treatment, employment, legal aid, or benefits programs operated by other organizations. This entry describes a navigation hub—staff here do not replace direct services at partner agencies.

15.description: Tennessee Department of Safety & Homeland Security — Driver Services serves justice-involved adults and families rebuilding stability after incarceration in throughout Tennessee. State agency providing REAL ID–compliant driver's licenses, photo identification cards, and reinstatement services at driver service centers across Tennessee. Reentry partners and TDOC assist returning citizens with documentation needed for employment and benefits. Online services available at tn.gov/safety for many transactions. Fees, proof of identity, and residency documents apply; reentry programs and legal aid partners often help gather required paperwork. Contact the program to confirm current intake steps, required documents, and whether walk-ins or referrals are accepted.
15.description_es: Tennessee Department of Safety & Homeland Security — Driver Services sirve a adultos con antecedentes penales y familias que reconstruyen estabilidad después de la encarcelación en en todo Tennessee. Aplican tarifas, prueba de identidad y documentos de residencia; programas de reinserción y aliados legales suelen ayudar a reunir el papeleo requerido. Contacte el programa para confirmar pasos de admisión, documentos requeridos y si aceptan visitas sin cita o referencias.

18.description: SAMHSA National Helpline is a statewide or regional referral resource for Tennesseans affected by the criminal justice system. Free confidential 24/7 treatment referral and information service for individuals and families facing mental health or substance use disorders. Provides referrals to local treatment facilities and community organizations in Tennessee and nationwide. Spanish-language support available through trained specialists. Users can search by location, service type, or need to find active providers in throughout Tennessee. Specialists or online tools help match people to housing, treatment, employment, legal aid, or benefits programs operated by other organizations. 31.description: Davidson County Reentry Council is a regional reentry coalition serving Davidson County. Partner agencies, faith communities, and workforce organizations meet regularly to share resources and warm referrals for people leaving jail or prison, on probation or parole, and their families. Coordinators help returning citizens connect to housing, employment, treatment, legal aid, and benefits through local partners. This is primarily a networking and navigation council—not an emergency shelter, cash assistance program, or crisis line.
31.description_es: Davidson County Reentry Council es una coalición regional de reinserción que sirve condado de Davidson. Agencias aliadas, comunidades de fe y organizaciones laborales se reúnen para compartir recursos y referencias directas para personas que salen de la cárcel o prisión, en libertad condicional o vigilada, y sus familias. Los coordinadores ayudan a conectar a ciudadanos que regresan con vivienda, empleo, tratamiento, asistencia legal y beneficios a través de aliados locales. Es principalmente un consejo de red y navegación—no un refugio de emergencia, programa de efectivo ni línea de crisis.
31.eligibility: Justice-involved residents of Davidson County and family members seeking local reentry partner referrals; coalition does not determine partner agency eligibility.
31.eligibility_es: Residentes con antecedentes penales de condado de Davidson y familiares que buscan referencias de aliados locales de reinserción; la coalición no determina la elegibilidad de agencias aliadas.

40.description: Legal Aid Society — Nashville Office serves justice-involved adults and families rebuilding stability after incarceration in 6 Tennessee counties. Free civil legal aid for low-income Nashville-area residents including eviction defense, public benefits appeals, and family law affecting reentry stability. Income and household-size limits apply; call centralized intake 800-238-1443 (LAS) or county office before visiting. Representation is limited to civil legal matters—not active criminal cases. Income, household size, and offense type determine eligibility for record relief. Contact the program to confirm current intake steps, required documents, and whether walk-ins or referrals are accepted.
40.description_es: Legal Aid Society — Nashville Office ofrece asistencia legal civil gratuita o de bajo costo para residentes de bajos ingresos en 6 condados de Tennessee. Los abogados ayudan con sellado o eliminación de antecedentes, desalojos, beneficios y otros asuntos civiles—no casos penales activos. La representación se limita a asuntos legales civiles—no casos penales activos. Ingresos, tamaño del hogar y tipo de delito determinan la elegibilidad para alivio de antecedentes. Contacte la oficina para confirmar pasos de admisión, documentos requeridos y si aceptan visitas sin cita o referencias.

284.description: Mid-South Food Bank — Partner Pantry Network serves justice-involved adults and families rebuilding stability after incarceration in 21 Tennessee counties. Mid-South Food Bank supplies partner food pantries and mobile distributions across west Tennessee counties including many rural communities where county DHS is the only local pin. Returning citizens can use the online pantry locator to find the nearest distribution site. Food assistance only—not housing, employment, or legal services. Food assistance does not usually require a criminal-record disclosure; staff can help with SNAP applications and pantry locations by ZIP code. Contact the program to confirm current intake steps, required documents, and whether walk-ins or referrals are accepted.
